Pak Study MCQs · pages 101-150

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Name the wife of Abu Jehal who used to throw garbage on Holy Prophet (PBUH) whenever he passed by her house?

Correct Answer: Ummay Jamil
Show explanation
Explanation

Umm Jamil was an aunt-in-law of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, who is mentioned in the Quran.

She was Abu Sufyan's sister. 

2

The most learned wife of the Holy Prophet (ﷺ) was____?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ayesha R.A
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Ayesha was the 3rd wife and most learned of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths

Muhammad married Hazrat Ayesha when he was 53 years old and she was only six years old.

He had intercourse with Hazrat Ayesha when she was nine years old.

Hazrat Ayesha was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker 

She gives the title of Siddiqa, meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man

She Died at the age of 65 in Medina 

She was  buried in Jannat al-Baqi

Hazrat Aisha (R.A) was 18 years old when Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) died 
3

Which wife of the Holy Prophet (SAW) was a scholar?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ayesha (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ) was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker & Wife Of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

Aisha was married to Muhammad in Mecca in 624 CE, after the Battle of Badr.
Resting place Jannat al-Baqi, Medina

Hazrat Ayesha Remained alive 46 years after the demise of Muhammad (PBUH).

Siddiqa was the title of Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ) meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man

Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths


"Hazrat Muhammad PBUH Had 11th Wives.

1.       HAZRAT KHADEJA,

2.       HAZRAT SAUDA

3.       HAZRAT AYESHA

4.       HAZRAT HAFSA

5.       HAZRAT ZAINAB KHUZEMA

6.       HAZRAT UMM E SALMA

7.       HAZRAT ZAINAB BINT E JAHESH

8.       HAZRAT JAVERIA

9.       HAZRAT UMM E HABIBA

10    HAZRAT SAFIA

11.   HAZRAT MEMONA RA

4

Where does Jannah (Heaven) lies?

Correct Answer: Under Mother’s feet
Show explanation
Explanation

Prophet Muhammad rightly said, "Heaven is under the feet of your mothers," as mothers play a pivotal role in preparing their children to live a healthy and prosperous life that contributes to the beauty of earth and society.

1st Heaven met Adam (AS)

2nd Heaven met Isa (AS) and Yahya (AS)

3rd Heaven met Yusuf (AS)

4th Heaven met Idris (AS)

5th Heaven met Haroon (AS)

6th Heaven met Musa (AS)

7th Heaven met Ibrahim (AS)

8

The creation of heaven and earth is in which Surah?

Correct Answer: Surah Al Al Araf
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah Al Al Araf mentioned, "Allah created the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, in six days" (7:54).

Al-Araf is the 7th chapter of the Qur'an, with 206 verses. 

"Meccan surah"

No. of Rukus: 24

No. of Sajdahs: 1 (verse 206)

Other names: The Purgatory

****

ND7-5-2023


9

In how many days Allah created the heavens and the earth?

Correct Answer: six
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah Al Al Araf mentioned, "Allah created the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, in six days" (7:54).

Al-Araf is the 7th chapter of the Qur'an, with 206 verses. 

"Meccan surah"

No. of Rukus: 24

No. of Sajdahs: 1 (verse 206)

Other names: The Purgatory

ND7-5-2023
10

Who met Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) at the first heaven during Miraj?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Adam
Show explanation
Explanation

The Miraj event took place on the 27th of Rajab in the 10th (621 A.D) year of Prophethood.

Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was staying at the house of Umm Hani on the night of Miraj.

That night, Angel Jibreel came to the Prophet (SAW) and took him on the journey of Israa and Miraj.

During Miraj, Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) met at the first heaven.

The event is mentioned in Surah Bani Israel.

It is also known as 'Isra and Mi'raj' or 'Shab-e-Meraj'. 

  • Isra: Night journey from Makkah to Masjid Al-Aqsa.
  • Mi’raj: Journey from Masjid Al-Aqsa to the heavens.

Gifts of the 27th of Rajab:

  • The five prescribed daily prayers.
  • The last part of the chapter of Al-Baqara (Amanar-Rasulu).

11

Hazrat Musa (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ______ heaven?

Correct Answer: 6th
Show explanation
Explanation

Who met with Holy Prophet PBUH on the Haven?

1st Heaven met Adam (AS)

3rd Heaven met Yusuf (AS)

5th Heaven met Haroon (AS)

6th Heaven met Musa (AS)

Hazrat Idrees (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ________ heaven?

13

How many categories of Mahram (Prohibited relations in marriage) exist?

Correct Answer: 3
Show explanation
Explanation

Islamic law recognizes three primary categories of mahram (prohibited relations in marriage):

  • Consanguinity (blood relations),
  • Affinity (relations by marriage)
  • Fosterage (milk kinship).

These categories establish permanent prohibitions on marriage to specific individuals.

14

Asma al-Rijal refers to what?

Correct Answer: Biographical evaluation of Hadith narrators
Show explanation
Explanation

Asma al-Rijal (Arabic: أسماء الرجال) refers to a biographical evaluation of Hadith narrators.

This Islamic discipline involves studying the names and biographies of the individuals who transmitted hadith (sayings and traditions of the Prophet Muhammad) to determine their reliability and credibility. 


15

Name the pillar of Islamic Civilization?

Correct Answer: Salat, Sa’um, Zakt and Hajj
Show explanation
Explanation

There are five pillars of Islam:

The first pillar of Islam is ____?

  Shahadatain 

The second pillar of Islam is------?

  Namaz

the third fundamental pillar of Islam?

  Zakat

The fourth pillar of Islam is--------?

 Fasting (Soam)

**

5th pillar of Islam is?

  Hajj

****

Which pillar of Islam is declared as armor?

 Fasting (Soam)

Which pillar of Islam washes sin like water?

  Hajj

16

What is the number of Surah Al-Mu’minoon in the Holy Quran?

Correct Answer: 23
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah Al-Mu’minoon is the 23rd Surah of the Holy Quran.

It consists of 118 verses.

It emphasizes the qualities of true believers.

17

Literal meaning of "Iblees" is:

Correct Answer: Disappointed one
Show explanation
Explanation

The meaning of the name Iblees is disappointed in God's mercy,

خدا کی رحمت سے مایوس، شیطان، خبیث

18

Which Surah of the Holy Quran describes the most concise description of Tauheed?

Correct Answer: Surah Ikhlas
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah Ikhlas gives the most concise and clear description of Tauheed (Oneness of Allah).

It emphasizes that Allah is One, eternal, and unique, without any partner or equal.

19

Who ordered throwing Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) into fire?

Correct Answer: Namrud
Show explanation
Explanation

Namrud ordered throwing Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) into the fire

Nimrod, the king of Babylon, commanded his men to throw Prophet Ibrahim (AS) into a huge pit of burning fire

Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained on fire for 40 days

Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)

Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born Iraq

Hazrat Ibrahim's (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.

Abraham had two children,

1.      Isaac

2.      Ismael,

****

20

Which Prophet was thrown into fire by his own people but did not burn?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ibrahim (AH)
Show explanation
Explanation

The name of the Prophet Ibrahim has been mentioned 69 times in Quran.
Nimrod, who was the King of Babylon put Hazrat Ibrahim in the fire.

21

Which of the following is fasting in Ramadan, the ninth month of the Islamic Year?

Correct Answer: Sawm
Show explanation
Explanation

Fasting during the month of Ramadan was made obligatory (wajib) during the month of Sha'ban (8th Month)

Fasting was the 4th fundamental pillar of Islam

Fasting (Soam)  is declared as armor

Fasting was made obligatory in 10th Shaban,2 A.H

The function of fasting is To Make the Sacrifice of Body and Soul

Saum (fasting) resembles Hadith with a Shield

5 days are prohibited ممنوع for fasting throughout the Year
22

The voluntary donations that Muslims may choose to give (in addition to Zakah) is called?

Correct Answer: Sadaqah
Show explanation
Explanation

Sadaqah means وہ چیز جو خدا کے نام پر دی جائے

Sadaqah refers to voluntary donations that Muslims give out of kindness, apart from the obligatory Zakah.

It is given anytime and in any amount.

23

_____ was the wife of Prophet Ibrahim (AS).

Correct Answer: Hajra (RH)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) had two wives:

  1. Sarah
  2. Hajra

Sarah was his first wife.

Hajra was his second wife, with whom he had a son, Ismail.

24

Bait-e-Rizwan (Pledge of the Tree) took place in ______ Hijrah?

Correct Answer: 6 A.H
Show explanation
Explanation

Bait-e-Rizwan/Pledge of the Tree was made 628/6 AH.

Bait-e-Rizwan took place in 6th Hijrah.

Bait Rizwan was the pledge taken to get revenge on Hazrat Usman (RA) under the tree known as Rizwan

Bait-e-Rizwan is also known as Bait-e-Shajra made under the Keekar tree.

On this occasion, Muhammad gathered his nearly 1,400 Sahaba and called them to pledge to fight until death and revenge the death of Hazrat Usman

******

25

In how many years Makki Surah were revealed?

Correct Answer: 13
Show explanation
Explanation

The number of Makki Surah is 86.

13 years Makki Surah was revealed.

The total number of Surah in the Holy Quran is 114.

 The number of Madni is 28.

There are 11 Sajdah are there in Makkah Surah.

There are only 03 Sajdah are there in Medinan Surah.

26

Which Surah of the Holy Quran revealed twice in Makkah and Madina?

Correct Answer: Surah Fatiha
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Fātiḥah was revealed twice, once in Makkah and later in Madina.

Al-Fātiḥah is the first chapter of the Quran.

Its seven verses are a prayer for the guidance, lordship and mercy of God.

33

What is the original name of Iblis?

Correct Answer: Azazil
Show explanation
Explanation

Azazil is usually seen as the original name of Iblis

The word 'Iblis' (إِبْلِيس) comes from the word Balasa (بَلَسَ), which means 'to despair'

Azazil is the name of Satan because he despairs from all goodness

liblis is the leader of the devils (shayāṭīn) in Islam.

 Iblis was thrown out of heaven after he refused to prostrate himself before Adam

Correct Answer: 2

Hazrat Adam A.S had 3 sons and 2 daughters.

The youngest son of Hazrat Adam A.S was Prophet Shees.

Known for: First human being

The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was 90 Feet

Hazrat Adam (AS) was 130 years of age when Shees alaihissalam was born

35

In which battle did the Holy Prophet (SAW) take part for the first time? Battle of Badar, Battle of Uhad, Harb-ul-Fijar, None of these

Correct Answer: Harb-ul-Fijar
Show explanation
Explanation

Harb-ul-Fijar (the Sacrilegious War) was the first battle in which the Holy Prophet (SAW) participated in his youth.

It took place between the Quraish and the Hawazin tribes before Prophethood.

36

Holy Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) performed the funeral of which king?

Correct Answer: Najashi
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Holy Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) performed an absentee funeral prayer (Ṣalāt al-Ġāʾib) for Najashi, the king of Abyssinia, after his death in 630 CE.
  • This was a sign of respect and recognition of Najashi's kindness and support for the Muslim emigrants who sought refuge in his kingdom.
37

How old was Hazrat Yousaf (peace be upon him) at the time of migration to Egypt?

Correct Answer: Almost seventeen years
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Yousaf (peace be upon him) was approximately 17 years old when he was taken from the well 

Important Question about "Hazrat Yousaf (AS)

Prophet Yusuf (عليه السلام) was one of the twelve sons of Prophet Yaqub (AS).

His mother's name was Raheel.

Hazrat Yousaf (AS) remained in the well for three days.

27 times Hazrat Yousuf was mentioned in the Holy Quran. 

Hazrat Yousaf (AS) was sold as a slave in Egypt.

Zulaikha bought Hazrat Yousaf (AS)

The real brother of Hazrat Yousaf A.S was Bin Yameen.

Hazrat Yousaf's (AS) talent for interpreting dreams.

Palestine is the grave of Hazrat Yousaf (علیہ).

38

Prophet Sulaiman (PBUH) was the youngest son of which prophet _________?

Correct Answer: Prophet Dawood (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Suleman A.S was the youngest son of the Prophet Dawud (A.S).

The name of his mother was Saba."

Prophet Hazrat Sulaiman Knew The Language Of Birds.

Bird Hud-Hud told Hazrat Sulaiman that “The Queen Balqees and her followers had Sun worshipping

Sulaiman (A.S) founded the famous Mosque Al-Aqsa

He was born in Jerusalem.


39

Give the name of the prophet who is the son of Hazrat Daud (AS)?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Suleiman (as)
Show explanation
Explanation

Prophet Sulaiman (PBUH) was the youngest son of Prophet Dawood (PBUH)

He was born in Jerusalem.

Allah (SWT) bestowed him the ability to speak to animals and control Jinns

His title is King of Israel

The Resting place is Al-Ḥaram ash-Sharīf, Jerusalem

40

Which was the favourite month of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ?

Correct Answer: Sha’ban
Show explanation
Explanation

Shaʽban is the eighth month of the Islamic calendar.
It is the month of "separation", so called because the pagan Arabs used to disperse in search of water.
The fifteenth night of this month is known as the "Night of Records". However, observance of this day is disputed.
41

Who was not born without a mother and a father? Jesus Christ (AS), Hazrat Adam (AS), Hazrat Moses (AS), Eve (AS)

Correct Answer: Hazrat Moses (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Moses (AS) was born naturally to parents.

Hazrat Adam (AS) and Eve (AS) were created directly by Allah, and Jesus Christ (AS) was born without a father.

42

Who was the first Katib-e-Wahi?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Sharjeel R.A
Show explanation
Explanation
Sharjeel bin Hasana — First prescriber of Wahi in Makkah.
Khalid bin Saeed — First writer of Wahi among Quraysh.
43

Ashab e Kahf slept for how many years?

Correct Answer: 309
Show explanation
Explanation

The word "kahf" means "the cave in the mountain"

the sura is named "kahf" because it speaks about the story of the Companions of the Cave

Ashab e Kahf slept for 309 years

The number of Ashab-e-Kahf was 7

The story of Ashab al-Kahf is narrated in the eighteenth chapter of the Quran, called “al-Kahf”  In verses 9-26

سورہ کہف میں اصحاب کہف کے ساتھ کتا جانور کا ذکر آیا ہے

Qitmeer is the name of the dog in Surah Kahf.

44

منافق کی ایک صفت یہ ہے؟

Correct Answer: وعدہ توڑنا ہے
Show explanation
Explanation

قرآن پاک میں منافقین کی نشانیوں میں سے ایک وعدہ خلافی بھی ہے۔

حدیث میں آیا ہے: "منافق کی تین نشانیاں ہیں: جب بولے جھوٹ بولے، جب وعدہ کرے تو خلاف کرے، جب اس کے پاس سامان رکھا جائے تو خیانت کرے" (بخاری)۔

45

The statement "Allah is creator, protector, provider, لاشریک" refers to _____?

Correct Answer: Toheed bi Siffat
Show explanation
Explanation

Meaning of Toheed bi Siffat:

It means Oneness of Allah in His Attributes, affirming that only Allah possesses perfect attributes like Creator (Al-Khaliq), Protector (Al-Hafiz), and Provider (Ar-Razzaq).

The listed qualities (creator, protector, provider) are divine attributes (Siffat) of Allah, and "لاشریک" emphasizes that He has no partners in these attributes.

46

عالم برزخ کسے کہتے ہیں؟

Correct Answer: موت اور قیامت کے دن کا درمیانی وقفہ
Show explanation
Explanation

دنیا اور آخرت کے درمیان ایک اور عالَم ہے جس کو برزخ کہتے ہیں 

مرنے کے بعد اور قیامت سے پہلے تمام اِنس وجن کو حسبِ مراتب اُس میں رہنا ہوتا ہے، اور یہ عالَم اِس دنیا سے بہت بڑا ہے۔

دنیا کے ساتھ برزخ کو وہی نسبت ہے جو ماں کے پیٹ کے ساتھ دنیا کوبرزخ میں کسی کو آرام ہے اور کسی کو تکلیف

47

How many Cows, Buffaloes or Bulls render one to pay Zakat?

Correct Answer: 30-40
Show explanation
Explanation

Zakat on Cows, Buffaloes, or Bulls:

Applies when they are raised for milk or breeding and graze freely.

Nisab: Minimum 30 animals.

30–39 animals: 1 one-year-old calf (male or female).

40–59 animals: 1 two-year-old cow.

48

What is the meaning of Quranic surah Al-Asr?

Correct Answer: Time
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah Al Asr Meaning is Time

Surah Al Asr is Makki Surah In 30th Para Of Holy Quran.

No of Verses;3

49

First _____ Aayat of Holy Quraan are called Tawwal?

Correct Answer: Seven
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Fātiḥah; meaning: "The Opening" or "The Opener".

Surah Fatihah is the first chapter (surah) of the Quran. Its seven verses (āyah).
50

Haq Mehr to be paid delightfully is mentioned in which Surah?

Correct Answer: Surah An-Nisa
Show explanation
Explanation

The instruction for Haq Mehr to be given willingly and with good cheer is mentioned in Surah An-Nisa, Verse 4.

The verse emphasizes that Mehr should be paid delightfully as a duty, and it should not be taken back once given.

51

Al-Maida means _______?

Correct Answer: The Table Spread with Food
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Maida Surah
No. of Rukus 16
No. of Verses 120
No. of Sajdahs 0
52

A person who recites Kalma of Islam Verbally Not Heartilly is called?

Correct Answer: Munafiq
Show explanation
Explanation

A person who reads the Kalima of Islam verbally and calls himself Muslim but disbelieves it heartily is called a “Hypocrite” (Munafiq).

Munafiq is a person who in public and in the community shows that he is a Muslim but rejects Islam 

In Islam, the munafiq or false Muslims or false believers.

The hypocrisy itself is called nifāq (نفاق).

53

Name the Angel who takes out souls of life bearing creatures?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Izraeel (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Izra’eel (AS) the Angel who takes out souls of life-bearing creatures

***

Izra'il – The Angel of Death, who takes the souls from bodies when people die.

Israfil – The angel who will be present on the day of resurrection.

Mīkāl_ is often depicted as providing nourishment for bodies and souls while also being responsible for bringing rain and thunder to Earth.

54

The Quranic surah Al-Ikhlas means ____?

Correct Answer: The Purity
Show explanation
Explanation

It is a short declaration of tawhid, Allah's absolute oneness, consisting of four ayat.

Al-Ikhlas means “the purity” or “the refining”.
55

Zakat is given on Camels?

Correct Answer: 5 camels
Show explanation
Explanation

The amount of zakat paid on capital assets (e.g. money) and stock-in-trade and jewelry is customarily 2.5% 

How many camels render are to par Zakat? 5

How many goats render are to pay zakat? 40

How many sheep render are to pay zakat? 40

Zakat is payable on gold of_________7.5 tolas

Zakat is payable on silver of_______52.5 tolas

 

56

Torah is the holy book of which Religion?

Correct Answer: Both A & B
Show explanation
Explanation

Torah is the holy book of Jews & Judaism.

The Torah  is the oldest Holy Book.

The Torah is the holy book of Jewish (the first five books of the Hebrew Bible)

which was revealed by God to Moses on Mount Sinai over 3,000 years ago.

Torah is also Judaism’s most important text.

تورات یہودیوں کی مقدس کتاب ہے (عبرانی بائبل کی پہلی پانچ کتابیں)
جو 3,000 سال پہلے کوہ سینا پر خدا کی طرف سے موسیٰ پر نازل ہوئی تھی۔
تورات بھی یہودیت کا سب سے اہم متن ہے۔

The first revelation on the holy prophet (PBUH) happened at which place?

Correct Answer: Cave Hira

610AD

5

Name the first prescriber of wahi in Makkah.

Sharjeel-ibn-Hasana Kandi

***

Who was the first writer of “Wahi” in Quraish?

58

Which is the first Source of Shariah?

Correct Answer: The Holy Quran
Show explanation
Explanation

Shariah is the legal practice derived from the teachings of the Quran, Islam's holy book, and the teachings of the Prophet Muhammad, or Sunnah.

59

Who was the first male martyr( Shaheed) of Islam?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Haris bin Halla (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Hazrat Haris bin Halla (RA) is the first male martyr (Shaheed) of Islam.
  • Sumayyah became the first ever shaheed, the first martyr in Islam.
  • Sumayyah's husband Yasir was soon killed as well, and he became the second shaheed in men. he was the overall (men and women) third shaheed in Islam.


    60

    How many rakats are there in Eid ul fitr prayer?

    Correct Answer: 2
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Eid literally means a “festival” or “feast” in Arabic. 

    Doing ghusl on Eid is mustahabb. 

    It was narrated that the Prophet ﷺ did ghusl on the day of Eid. 

    Eid Prayer is Wajib

    Eid Salat consists of two 'Rakats' and six 'Takbirs'.

    There are two Eid prayers in a year, Eid-ul-fitr and Eid-ul-adha.

    first Eid Prayer offered by the Holy prophet in 2nd A.H

    ND18-2-2023

    61

    The literal meaning of Zakat is to -----.

    Correct Answer: increase
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The literal meaning of Zakat is to increase.
    • **
    • The literal meaning of Zakat is to increase, technical meaning is purification.
    • **
    • A related question about Zakat
    • Zakat is the 3rd fundamental pillar of Islam.
    • Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H but its complete system was imposed in the year of 8th hijrah.
    • Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) said that there was no difference between Salat and Zakat.
    • The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.
    • Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat.
    • Without which act the prayer is useless? Zakat
    • One who disbelievers in Zakat is a Kafir
    • **
    • A person who is liable to pay Zakat is called………… Sahib-e-Nisab
    • **
    • How many camels render are to par Zakat? 5
    • How many goats render are to pay zakat? 40
    • How many cows render are to pay Zakat? 30-40
    • How many bulls render to pay Zakat? 30-40
    • How may buffaloes render are to pay zakat? 30-40
    • **
    • Zakat is payable on gold of_________7.5 tolas
    • Zakat is payable on silver of_______52.5 tolas
    • **
    • “Zakat” is the treasure of Islam, who said this? Holy Prophet (PBUH)
    • How many times word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs? 22 times
    • Who is not entitled to get Zakat? Parents, Husband, wife, and children
    • **
    62

    Total number of rakats in Farz prayers is?

    Correct Answer: 17
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The total number of obligatory (Fard) rakats for the five daily prayers is 17:

    2 for Fajr,

    4 for Dhuhr,

    4 for Asr,

    3 for Maghrib, 

    4 for Isha.  

    63

    Who introduced punctuation marks in the Holy Quran?

    Correct Answer: Hujjaj bin Yousuf
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hujjaj bin Yousuf added punctuation marks in the Holy Quran.

    Punctuation marks in the glorious Quran are called Alamatul Waqf or stopping punctuation.

     

    64

    When Wazu (Ablution) was ordered?

    Correct Answer: 5 A.H
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Wazu (Ablution) was ordered on  5 A.H

    The acts which break the ablution are called Nawaqiz-e-Wazu

    There are 6 Sunnah acts of ablution

    There are 4 obligations for ablution.

    13 Sunnah are in four obligations of Ablution

    By Hazrat Jibrail (علیہ), Prophet ﷺ learnt Salat & ablution

    وضو کے چار فرائض ہیں۔

    نمبر 1: چہرہ دھونا

    نمبر 2: دونوں ہاتھوں کو کہینیوں تک دھونا

    نمبر 3: چوتھائی سر کا مسح کرنا

    نمبر 4:پاوں کا ٹخنیوں سمیت دھونا

    ****

    Pillars of Islam are called _____?

      Shahadatain

      Namaz

    the third fundamental pillar of Islam?

      Zakat

    The fourth pillar of Islam is--------?

     Fasting (Soam)

    5th pillar of Islam is ?

      Hajj

    Which pillar of Islam is declared as armor?

    Which pillar of Islam washes sin like water?

    68

    Which Prophet (A.S) was the carpenter by profession?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Zakariya A.S
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Prophet Zakariya (peace be upon him) was the patron of Mary.

    He was the trustee of Hekal.

    He worked as a carpenter and earned his livelihood.

    The Prophet Zakariya (peace be upon him) kept the torch of Mosaic law burning.

    ****

    More Details
    Prophet Idris (A.S) was a tailor,
    Prophet Nuh (A.S) built the Ark,
     Prophet Isa (A.S) was a preacher and healer.

    69

    "Baa" in the word "Bismillah" stands for which preposition?

    Correct Answer: By/In
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Arabic "Baa" (بِ) in "Bismillah" can mean with, in, or by depending on context.

    It reflects seeking help or blessing through Allah's name.

    70

    How many Mosques are mentioned in the Holy Quran?

    Correct Answer: 5
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Five Mosques are named in Quran.

    1. Masjid-ul-Haram
    2. Masjid-ul-Aqsa
    3. Masjid-ul-Zarrar
    4. Masjid-ul-Nabaw
    5. Masjid Quba

    ****

    71

    How many numbers of Surah in the last Para of Holy Quran?

    Correct Answer: 37
    Show explanation
    Explanation
     The last para of the Holy Quran, Juz Amma, contains the following 37 surahs:
    1. Surah Al-Naba (Chapter 78)
    2. Surah Al-Nazi'at (Chapter 79)
    3. Surah Abasa (Chapter 80)
    4. Surah At-Takwir (Chapter 81)
    5. Surah Al-Infitar (Chapter 82)
    6. Surah Al-Mutaffifin (Chapter 83)
    7. Surah Al-Inshiqaq (Chapter 84)
    8. Surah Al-Buruj (Chapter 85)
    9. Surah At-Tariq (Chapter 86)
    10. Surah Al-A'la (Chapter 87)
    11. Surah Al-Ghashiyah (Chapter 88)
    12. Surah Al-Fajr (Chapter 89)
    13. Surah Al-Balad (Chapter 90)
    14. Surah Ash-Shams (Chapter 91)
    15. Surah Al-Layl (Chapter 92)
    16. Surah Ad-Duha (Chapter 93)
    17. Surah Ash-Sharh (Chapter 94)
    18. Surah At-Tin (Chapter 95)
    19. Surah Al-Alaq (Chapter 96)
    20. Surah Al-Qadr (Chapter 97)
    21. Surah Al-Bayyinah (Chapter 98)
    22. Surah Az-Zalzalah (Chapter 99)
    23. Surah Al-Adiyat (Chapter 100)
    24. Surah Al-Qari'ah (Chapter 101)
    25. Surah At-Takathur (Chapter 102)
    26. Surah Al-Asr (Chapter 103)
    27. Surah Al-Humazah (Chapter 104)
    28. Surah Al-Fil (Chapter 105)
    29. Surah Quraysh (Chapter 106)
    30. Surah Al-Ma'un (Chapter 107)
    31. Surah Al-Kawthar (Chapter 108)
    32. Surah Al-Kafirun (Chapter 109)
    33. Surah An-Nasr (Chapter 110)
    34. Surah Al-Masad (Chapter 111)
    35. Surah Al-Ikhlas (Chapter 112)
    36. Surah Al-Falaq (Chapter 113)
    37. Surah An-Nas (Chapter 114)
    72

    What is Sahihain:

    Correct Answer: Bukhari and Muslim
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    6 books of Ahadis also known as sihah e sitta 

    Sahih Bukhari, collected by Imam Bukhari 

    Sahih Muslim, collected by Muslim 

    Sunan al-Sughra, collected by al-Nasa'i 

    Sunan Abu Dawood, collected by Abu Dawood 

    Jami al-Tirmidhi, collected by al-Tirmidhi 

    Sunan ibn Majah, collected by Ibn Majah

    73

    What was the real name of Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A)?

    Correct Answer: Abdullah ibn Uthman
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Abu Bakr real name was Abdullah Ibn Abi Quhafa.

    He was a sahabi, one of the companions of Muhammad and the first Caliph of Islam.

    He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.

    His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya

    Hazrat Abu Bakar was a merchant of Cloth

    Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) Died in 13th Hijra


    74

    What is the meaning of the name “Murtaza”, which is one of the titles associated with the Holy Prophet (PBUH) and Imam Ali (AS)?

    Correct Answer: The Chosen One
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The name "Murtaza" (مرتضیٰ) is indeed an Arabic word meaning "The Chosen One" or "the favored one"

    It is a highly revered title and a common honorific used for Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS)

    The cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH),

    And the first Imam in Shia Islam and the fourth Caliph in Sunni Islam. 


    75

    Who was the youngest son of Hazrat Adam (A.S)?

    Correct Answer: Sheesh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Adam A.S had 3 sons and 2 daughters.

    The youngest son of Hazrat Adam A.S was Prophet Shees.

    Qabil (Cain): He was the elder son

    Habil (Abel): He was the younger brother 

    76

    Joseph is used for which Prophet?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Yusuf (A.S.)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In English, "Joseph" is the name used for Hazrat Yusuf (A.S.), one of the sons of Hazrat Yaqoob (A.S.).

    Prophet Yusuf (عليه السلام) was one of the twelve sons of Prophet Yaqub (AS).

    His mother name was Raheel, (Rachel).

    What was the Religion of Hazrat Adam?

    Correct Answer: Islam
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Adam was the first Muslim, as the Quran, states that all the Prophets preached the same faith of Islam.

    Known for: First human being

    Spouse(s): Hawwā (حواء)

    The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was 90 Feet

    Hazrat Adam (AS) was 130 years of age when Shees alaihissalam was born

    The language is spoken by Adam in the Garden of Eden.

    What was the Religion of Hazrat Adam?

    930 years✅

    78

    How many infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr?

    Correct Answer: 70
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr


    All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

    Badr means in Arabic: Full moon

    Furqan the second name of the battle of Badr

    Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the battle of Badar.

    The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam 

    The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.

    It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

    14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

    No of the Kufars (Non-Muslims Forces) in the Battle of Badr were 1000

     mentioned in Surat Al-Anfal 

    1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

    Total: Muslim 313-317

    Muhajirun: 82-85

    Ansar: 231

    Aws: 61

    Khazraj: 170

    2 horses

    70 camels

    **

    ND23-2-2023

    79

    In Naml two bismillah occur (2nds one is at aayat no:______)

    Correct Answer: 30
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Surah Al Naml of holy Quran Bismillah is added

    Surah Naml, which is the 27th Chapter, with 93 verses

    the entire bismillahir rahmanir Raheem in the 30th verse.

    This is why Surah Naml is known as the Surah with two bismillah.

    Surah Naml is an earlier "Meccan surah",

    while

    Surah Al-Toba starts without “Bismillah”

    Bismillahir Rahmaanir Raheem is repeated in the Holy Quran  114 Times

    ND16-3-2023

    Hazrat Abdul Mutalib suggested the Holy Prophet (PBUH) name as Muhammad SAW.

    Hazrat Amina suggested the Holy Prophet (PBUH) name as Ahmed.

    Ramzan is the _______ month of Islamic calendar?

    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Ramzan is the 9th month of Islamic calendar.
    • Fast was obligatory in 2nd Hijri.
    • Ramadan is divided into three stages-
    • First Ashra (Rehmat),
    • Second Ashra (Maghfirat)
    • Third Ashra (Nijaat).

    84

    What is meant by Rami al-Jamarat?

    Correct Answer: Stoning the pillars representing Satan
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Rami al-Jamarat is a mandatory Hajj ritual
    • involving the symbolic stoning of three pillars in Mina
    • 10th Dhu al-Hijjah: Only the Jamrat al-Aqabah (the largest pillar) is stoned.
    • 11th & 12th Dhu al-Hijjah: All three pillars (Small, Medium, Large) are stoned in order.
    • The act of throwing stones at the Jamarat is known as “Rami”.

    Last updated by NA on 26 April 2026

    85

    How many sons and daughters of Prophet (PBUH) had?

    Correct Answer: 3 sons and 4 daughters
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are Four Daughters of the Prophet (PBUH).

    1. Hazrat Zainab (RA) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ daughter had Khadija)
    2. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ daughter had Khadija)
    3. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ daughter had Khadija)
    4. Hazrat Fatima (RA) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ daughter had Khadija)

    Three Sons of Prophet (PBUH)

    1. Hazrat Al-Qasim was born from HAZRAT KHADIJA. buried in Jannat ul Moalla, Mecca
    2. Hazrat Abdullah was born from HAZRAT KHADIJA. buried in Jannat ul Moalla, Mecca
    3. Hazrat Ibrahim was the third son and born from Hazrat Maria. Buried in Jannat ul Baki, Madina.

    Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026


    86

    How many times did Muhammad PBUH perform hajj?

    Correct Answer: 1
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Prophet ﷺ performed Only one Hajj in life 

    The Holy Prophet performed hajj in 10 Hijri

    It was Muhammad ؓﷺ first and last Haj.

    This Hajj of Muhammad ؓﷺ is known as Hujjat al-wada

    Additional information

    Muhammad ؓﷺ performed 4 umrah 

    The first Umrah Prophet Muhammad made after the Migration to Medina. on 7 AH (629 CE)

    Total number of surah in Quran are ____?

    Total No Of Verses Are 6666
    Total No Of Ruku     540/558
    Total No Of     Sajida’s 14

    Each of the 114 surahs, which vary in length from several pages to several words, encompasses one or more revelations received by Muhammad from Allah (God).

    88

    Which festival is celebrated after the month of Ramzan?

    Correct Answer: Eid al-Fitr
    Show explanation
