    Eid al-Fitr marks the end of Ramadan,

    and is celebrated during the first three days of Shawwal,

    89

    Which famous Ghazwa is mentioned in Surah Al-Imran?

    Correct Answer: Ghazwah Badar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ghazwa Badr is mentioned in Surah Aal-e-Imran (Verses 123–125).
    However, Ghazwa Uhud is also discussed later (Verses 121–180).


    More Details
    The Battle of Badr took place in 2 A.H (624 AD) near Madinah, the first major victory of Islam.
    The Battle of Uhud     (3 A.H) is discussed for moral and strategic lessons.

    سورۃ آلِ عمران میں غزوہ بدر کا ذکر آیات 123 تا 125 میں اور غزوہ اُحد کا تفصیلی ذکر آیات 121 تا 180 میں آیا ہے۔ غزوہ بدر مسلمانوں کی پہلی بڑی فتح تھی۔


    چونکہ دونوں غزوات اس صورت میں ہیں تو درست جواب کیا ہوگا

    the correct answer is Ghazwa Badr,
    because that’s where Allah directly mentions the name and victory of Badr.


    Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026

    90

    وٹَر فرض نماز ہے؟

    Correct Answer: غلط
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    علی رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت ہے وہ فرماتے ہیں کہ وتر فرض نمازوں کی طرح واجب نہیں ہے؛ بلکہ یہ تو رسول اللہ ﷺ کی ایک سنت ہے۔

    91

    The father name of the last Holy Prophet SAW is ________?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abdullah
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Hazrat Abdullah was married to Aminah bint Wahb.
    Muhammad ﷺ was their only offspring.
    Muhammad'ﷺ father, Abdullah, died almost six months before he was born
    Hazrat Abdullah was Died in c. 570 (aged 23–24) Medina, Hejaz
    The resting place of Hazrat Abdullah  is Jannat al-Baqi, Medina


    92

    How many holy books are there in Islam?

    Correct Answer: 4
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are four Holy Books

    1. The Torat  revealed to Musa
    2. The Zabur revealed to Dawud 
    3. The Injil (Gospel) revealed to Isa 
    4. The Quran was revealed to Muhammad.
    93

    At age of ________ Holy Prophet SAW received first Wahi.

    Correct Answer: 40 Years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Muhammad's first revelation was an event described in Islamic tradition as taking place in 610 AD.

    The event took place in a cave Hira, located on the mountain Jabal an-Nour, near Mecca.

    These 5 verses are:

    اِقۡرَاۡ بِاسۡمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِىۡ خَلَقَ​ۚ‏ ﴿96:1﴾ خَلَقَ الۡاِنۡسَانَ مِنۡ عَلَقٍ​ۚ‏ ﴿96:2﴾ اِقۡرَاۡ وَرَبُّكَ الۡاَكۡرَمُۙ‏ ﴿96:3﴾ الَّذِىۡ عَلَّمَ بِالۡقَلَمِۙ‏ ﴿96:4﴾ عَلَّمَ الۡاِنۡسَانَ مَا لَمۡ يَعۡلَمۡؕ‏ ﴿96:5﴾

    94

    Ayat ul Kursi is in which Surah of the Holy Quran?

    Correct Answer: Surah Al-Baqarah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ayatul Kursi is the 255th verse of the 2nd surah of the Quran, Al-Baqarah.

    It is located in the third para named "تِلْكَ ٱلْرُّسُلُ."

    It is also known as Sardar ul Ayat or The Throne Verse.

    Surah Al-Baqarah is the longest Surah of the Qur'an.

    It was revealed after the Prophet ﷺ migrated to Madinah.


    Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026
    95

    Salat-e-Kasoof is offered at the time of ____?

    Correct Answer: Solar (Sun) eclipse
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Salat ul-Kusuf is a prayer of two rakaaht performed during a solar eclipse.

    Namaz-e-Khasoof (نماز خسوف) is offered during a lunar eclipse.

    Namaz-e-Kasoof (نماز کسوف) is offered during a solar eclipse.


    96

    At the time of Ghazwa-e-Khandaq there was only one Jewish tribe left in Madina name it?

    Correct Answer: Banu Quraizah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    During Ghazwa-e-Khandaq, Banu Quraizah was the only Jewish tribe remaining in Madina.

    They broke their treaty with the Muslims during the battle, leading to a significant confrontation afterward.

    97

    How many Countries are members of OIC?

    Correct Answer: 57
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    OIC Stands for Organization of Islamic Cooperation

    Total Current member= 57

    OIC headquarters is located in Jadda.

    It is the largest international organization outside of the United Nations.

    The official languages of the OIC are:

    1.  Arabic,
    2. English, 
    3. French.

    It was founded in 1969 and had 25 members originally.

    It changed its name on 28 June 2011 from the Organization of the Islamic Conference to its current name.

    The first summit of OIC was held in Rabat



    98

    Fatimid Caliphate was established in which country?

    Correct Answer: Egypt
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In 969, they conquered Egypt, and in 973 they established Cairo as the capital of their caliphate.

    The Fatimid Caliphate was an Ismaili Shia caliphate of the 10th to the 12th centuries CE.

    99

    Surah An-Nur describes the modesty of whom?

    Correct Answer: Both men and women
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Surah Al-Noor is found in the 18th Parah (Juz) of the Quran.

    It has 64 verses.

    It is divided into 9 Rukus.

    It focusing on social laws and morality for both men and women.

    100

    Jamila al-Azhar is located in which country?

    Correct Answer: Cairo
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Al-Azhar (both the mosque and university) is located in Cairo, Egypt.


    Founded in 970–972 AD 


    Founded by Jawhar al-Siqilly, the Fatimid conqueror 


    The first khutba was delivered from its minbar in 972.


    A university was established there in 988.

    101

    Talha ibn Ubaidullah got the title “The Living Martyr” after which Ghazwa?

    Correct Answer: Ghazwa-e-Uhud
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Talha ibn Ubaidullah (R.A) fought bravely in Ghazwa-e-Uhud.

    Despite being seriously wounded, he survived, earning the title “The Living Martyr (Shaheed-e-Hayy)”.

    102

    Ruma was the mother of _____?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Talha (R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ruma was the mother of Hazrat Talha (R.A), a famous companion of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.

    Hazrat Talha (R.A) is known for his bravery and generosity in early Islamic history.

    103

    What the period of Hazrat Usman (RA) as third caliph of Islam?

    Correct Answer: 12 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Usman (R.A)

    Belonged

    Quraysh

    Born

    Makkah

    Caliph

    Third caliph

    Caliphate

    23 to 35 Hijjah

    Caliphate

    644 to 656 CE

    Titles

    Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

    Martyred

    18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

    Martyrdom day

    Friday

    Wives

    Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

    Full Name

    Usman bin Affaan

    Death cause

    Martyred by rebels

    Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

    Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

    Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

    Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

    After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

    Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

    Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

    Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

    Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

     Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

    Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 

    104

    اسلامی اقدار کے مطابق عزت کا معیار کیا ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: تقوی
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    اسلام میں عزت کا اصل معیار تقویٰ ہے، اور یہ تمام دی ہوئی خصوصیات (حسنِ اخلاق، خدمتِ خلق، دیانت) کی بنیاد ہے۔

    عزت کا اصل پیمانہ اللہ کا خوف اور اس کی اطاعت ہے۔ جب انسان تقویٰ پر قائم ہوتا ہے تو وہ حسنِ اخلاق، خدمتِ خلق، اور دیانت جیسے اعمال انجام دیتا ہے، جو عزت کا باعث بنتے ہیں۔ 


    105

    کس عمل سے قوت برداشت میں اضافہ ہوتا ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: روزہ
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    روزہ وہ بنیادی عمل ہے جس سے انسان کی قوت برداشت اور قوت ارادی میں براہِ راست اضافہ ہوتا ہے。

    106

    آیت کریم ہے کہ عادل کا دمانّ چھوڑو چاہئے تمہارا

    Correct Answer: اپنا بھائی ہی کیوں نہ ہو
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    آیت کریم ہے کہ عادل کا دمانّ چھوڑو چاہئے تمہارا اپنا بھائی ہی کیوں نہ ہو 

    مندرجہ ذیل حوالہ قرآن مجید کی سورہ النساء کی آیت نمبر 135 کی جانب اشارہ ہے۔


    107

    قرآن پاک میں ارشاد ہے اپنی اولاد کو قتل نہ کرو؟

    Correct Answer: غربت اور افلاس کے ڈر سے
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    وَلَا تَقۡتُلُوۡۤا اَوۡلَادَكُمۡ خَشۡيَةَ اِمۡلَاقٍ​ؕ نَحۡنُ نَرۡزُقُهُمۡ وَاِيَّاكُمۡ​ؕ اِنَّ قَتۡلَهُمۡ كَانَ خِطۡاً كَبِيۡرًا‏ ٣١

    اور اپنی اولاد کو قتل نہ کرو مفلسی کے اندیشے سے ہم ان کو بھی رزق دیں گے اور تمہیں بھی یقیناً ان کو قتل کرنا بہت بڑی خطا ہے


    108

    اسلامی معیشت میں ریڑھ کی ہڈی کسے کہتے ہیں؟

    Correct Answer: زکوة
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    زکوٰۃ اسلامی معیشت میں ریڑھ کی ہڈی ہے

    109

    اسلام ایک دین ھے کیونکہ یہ

    Correct Answer: ضابطہ حیات ھے
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    اسلام کو دین اس لیے کہا جاتا ہے کیونکہ یہ صرف عبادات یا عقائد کا مجموعہ نہیں بلکہ ایک مکمل ضابطہ حیات  ہے۔

    یہ زندگی کے ہر شعبے کے لیے رہنمائی فراہم کرتا ہے — انفرادی، اجتماعی، معاشی، سیاسی اور اخلاقی۔

    110

    اسلام کا معاشی انصاف یہ ھے کہ

    Correct Answer: محنت کے مطابق دیا جاے
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    اسلام کا معاشی انصاف اس اصول پر قائم ہے کہ ہر فرد کو اس کی محنت، قابلیت، اور ضرورت کے مطابق دیا جائے۔  

    - اسلام سرمایہ داری کی طرح دولت کا ارتکاز نہیں چاہتا  

    - اور نہ ہی اشتراکیت کی طرح سب کچھ برابر بانٹتا ہے  

    - بلکہ اسلام کا نظام اعتدال پر مبنی ہے، جہاں محنت کا صلہ ملتا ہے اور کمزوروں کا خیال رکھا جاتا ہے (جیسے زکٰوة، صدقات وغیرہ کے ذریعے)

    111

    Hazrat ______ levied zakat on horses?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umer
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Umar ibn al-Khattab was the second caliph of the Rashidun Caliph from 634 to 644 CE.
    During the earlier period, there was a great dearth of horses.
    Most of the horses had been killed in early battles.
    In the time of Hazrat Uthman, things had changed.
    112

    From which Word ‘Aqeeda’ is Derived?

    Correct Answer: Aqd
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The word ‘Aqeeda’ is derived from the Arabic root ‘Aqd’, which means to tie or bind firmly.

    عقیدہ عربی لفظ "عَقد" سے نکلا ہے، جس کا مطلب ہے باندھنا یا مضبوطی سے باندھنا۔

    عقیدہ دل میں کسی بات پر پختہ یقین رکھنے کو کہتے ہیں، جیسے اللہ پر ایمان۔

    113

    Hazrat Younis (AS) remained in the abdomen of fish for how many days?

    Correct Answer: 40 days
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Younis remained in the abdomen of fish for 40 days.

    Surah Younis is the 10th chapter (surah) of the Quran with 109 verses (ayat).

    His named after the prophet Yunus (Jonah).

    He is mentioned in the holy Quran: 4 times

    Hazrat Younis (AS) is called Dhun-Nun 

     

    114

    The battle of Hunain was fought between ______?

    Correct Answer: The Prophet (PBUH) & his companions and Hawazin (Hawazin and Thaqif)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 1st Shawwal, 8th Hijra (630 CE), a force of 12,000 Muslims approached the valley of Hunain.

    The Battle of Hunain, fought near al-Ta'if.

    It was fought between the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and his companions against the Hawazin and Thaqif tribes.

    It took place shortly after the conquest of Makkah.

    The Battle of Hunain resulted in a Muslim victory over the Bedouin tribes.

    115

    Atique is the title of _____?

    Correct Answer: Abubakar
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Atique is the Title of Hazrat Abu Bakar (RA).
    • ..
    • Abu Bakr  was a sahabi, one of the companions of Muhammad and the first Caliph of Islam.
    • He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.
    • His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya.
    116

    Name of maternal grandmother of Prophet (PBUH) was _____?

    Correct Answer: Barrah bint Abd Al-Uzza
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    - Fatima bint Amr was the parental grandmother of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) from the father’s side.  
    - She belonged to the Makhzum clan of the Quraysh tribe.
    The maternal grandmother of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was Barrah bint Abd Al-Uzza.
    She was the mother of Amina bint Wahb (RA), the Prophet’s (PBUH) mother.

    Family of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ):

    Father: Hazrat Abdullah (R.A)

    Mother: Hazrat Amina (R.A)

    Grandfather: Hazrat Abdul Muttalib

    Grandmother: Fatimah bint Amr

    Uncle: Hazrat Abu Talib (chief guardian in youth)

    (Also: Hamza, Abbas, Abu Lahab — other uncles)

    117

    What was the name of Hazrat Muhammad parental grandmother?

    Correct Answer: Fatimah bint Amr
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    - Fatima bint Amr was the parental grandmother of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) from the father’s side.  
    - She belonged to the Makhzum clan of the Quraysh tribe.
    The maternal grandmother of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was Barrah bint Abd Al-Uzza.
    She was the mother of Amina bint Wahb (RA), the Prophet’s (PBUH) mother.

    Family of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ):

    Father: Hazrat Abdullah (R.A)

    Mother: Hazrat Amina (R.A)

    Grandfather: Hazrat Abdul Muttalib

    Grandmother: Fatimah bint Amr

    Uncle: Hazrat Abu Talib (chief guardian in youth)

    (Also: Hamza, Abbas, Abu Lahab — other uncles)

    118

    Tariq bin Ziyad conquered Spain the year?

    Correct Answer: Rajab, 92 Hijri (711 AD)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Tariq bin Ziyad initiated the Muslim conquest of Spain in 711 AD
    • Initiated the Muslim conquest of the Iberian Peninsula (present-day Spain and Portugal)
    • Against the Visigothic Kingdom in 711–718 AD.
    • Initial Landing: April 711 AD.
    • Decisive Battle: The Battle of Guadalete on July 19, 711 AD.

    Additional information 

    •  The Rock of Gibraltar is named after him ("Jabal Ṭāriq" - Mountain of Tariq).

    Last updated question by NS on 11 May 2026

    119

    Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) was died in ______?

    Correct Answer: 13th Hijra
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Abu Bakar (رضہ) Born: October 27, 573 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia

    Hazrat Abu Bakar (رضہ) Died: August 23, 634 AD, Medina, Saudi Arabia

    120

    Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA), the wife of Hazrat Usman (RA) died at victory day of which battle?

    Correct Answer: Badr
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ruqayyah (RA) died on the victory day of the Battle of Badr (in Ramadan 17, 2 AH)

    She was the Second daughter of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ 

    She married (2nd time) to the third caliph Uthman

    Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in the Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on his deathbed.

    She had only one son Abd Allah from Hazrat Usman (RA) but died at the age of six


    Additional Information:


    Hazrat Usman (RA) married two of the daughters of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) 

    After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

    Umm-e-Kulthum was the third daughter of Hazrat Muhammad 
    121

    The original name of the host of Holy Prophet Abu Ayub Ansari is _____?

    Correct Answer: Khalid bin Zaid (R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The original name of the host of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was Khalid bin Zaid (R.A).

    He is better known as Abu Ayub Ansari (R.A).

    He provided refuge to the Prophet during his migration to Madinah.

    122

    Which companion of the Prophet (PBUH) was known as the greatest scholar of the Quran?

    Correct Answer: Abdullah bin Abbas (RA)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Abdullah bin Abbas (RA) was renowned as the greatest scholar and interpreter of the Qur’an among the companions.

    The Prophet (PBUH) prayed for him saying, “O Allah, teach him the interpretation of the Qur’an.”

    123

    How many Doors of hell are there?

    Correct Answer: 7
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The doors of hell are seven.

    The gate keeper of hell is Malik

    The doors of heaven are eight.

    The gatekeeper of heaven is Rizwan.

    ****

    Name of 7 doors of hell:

    1. Jahannam was reserved for Muslims who had committed grave sins.
    2. al-Laza (the blaze) for the Jews.
    3. al-Hutama (the consuming fire) for the Christians.
    4. al-Sa'ir for the Sabians.
    5. al-Saqar (the scorching fire) for the Zoroastrians.
    6. al-jahim (the hot place) for the idolaters.
    7. al-Hawiya (the abyss) for the hypocrites.
    124

    How Hazrat Salman Farsi (RA) was freed as a slave?

    Correct Answer: Prophet (PBUH) and his companions carried out plantation in the garden in lieu thereof
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Salman (R.A.) was released from slavery by the Prophet (S.A.W.) who paid his Jewish slave-owner a stipulated price and who himself planted an agreed number of date palms to secure his manumission.

    Salman was a companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

    First Persian who converted to Islam.

    He was referred to by the kunya Abu Abdullah. 

    Born: 568 AD, Jay

    Died: Ctesiphon, Iraq


    125

    Who was the first ambassador of Islam?

    Correct Answer: Mus‘ab bin ‘Umair R.A
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Mus’ab ibn Umayr was appointed the first ambassador of Islam and was sent to Yathrib (Medina) to prepare the city for the forthcoming Hijra after the first pledge with the Ansar.

    A man of Medina named Asad ibn Zurarah assisted him.

    126

    Where is Hajr-e-Aswad situated?

    Correct Answer: Eastern Corner of Kaaba
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hajr-e-Aswad, or the Black Stone, is located in the eastern corner of the Kaaba in the Grand Mosque in Mecca, Saudi Arabia.
    • The Black Stone is a revered Islamic relic believed to have been set into the Kaaba by the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) in 605 CE, five years before his first revelation.
    127

    Prophet (PBUH) married to Hazrat Juwayriah Bint Harith (RA) after which battle?

    Correct Answer: Ghazwa to Banu Mustaliq
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Prophet (PBUH) married Hazrat Juwayriah bint al-Harith (RA) after the Battle of Banu Mustaliq.

    Her marriage led to the freedom of many captives, strengthening ties between Muslims and her tribe.

    128

    Verse 22 of Surah Mujadillah was revealed due to the following incident?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Ubeydah (RA) killed his father in the Battle of Badr
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Verse 22 of Surah Al-Mujadilah praises believers who stand firm in faith even against their own relatives.

    It was revealed after Hazrat Abu Ubaydah (RA) killed his idolater father during the Battle of Badr for the sake of Islam.

    129

    Which surah ends with two Prophets name?

    Correct Answer: Surah Al-Ala
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Holy Quran has 25 Prophets names mentioned and there are 6 Surahs titled with their names.

    1.           Surah Ibrahim
    2.           Surah Yusuf
    3.      Surah Mohammed
    4.           Surah Nuh
    5.        Surah Hood
    6.          Surah Younus (Jonah).

    Surah Al-A’la ends with which two prophets' names? Hazrat Ibrahim & Musa

    ND7-5-2023


    130

    How many Surahs of the Holy Quran start with Al-Hamdulillah?

    Correct Answer: Five
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are the following Five Surahs of the Holy Quran start with Al-Hamdulillah.

    1: al Fatiha
    2: a Al An'am
    3: Al Kahf
    4: Saba
    5: Fatir All are Makki
    131

    How many surahs of the Holy Quran start with the word Ha-meem?

    Correct Answer: 7
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are seven surah start Ha-meem

    1.      Surah Ghafir,

    2.      Surah Fussilat,

    3.      Surah Shuraa,

    4.      Surah Zukhruf,

    5.      Surah Dukhan,

    6.      Surah Jathiyah  

    7.      Surah Ahqaf.
    132

    Victory in Ghazwa-e-Mustaliq, also triggered internal tensions among the Muslim ranks, leading to the revelation of which surah?

    Correct Answer: Surah Al-Munafiqun
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    After the Ghazwa-e-Mustaliq, hypocrisy and internal conflict appeared among Muslims.

    Surah Al-Munafiqun was revealed to expose the hypocrites (Munafiqeen) and warn against disunity.

    133

    Hazrat Yousaf (AS) and Hazrat Yaqoob (AS) met each other after a period of how many years?

    Correct Answer: 40 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Yousaf (AS) and Hazrat Yaqoob (AS) met each other after a period of 40 years

    Hazrat Yousaf (AS) was put into the jail by the king of Egypt. He remained behind the bars for  5 years

    Hazrat Yousaf (AS) remained in the well for 3 DAYS 

    Yaqub was also known as Israel.

    Prophet Yusuf (AS) was the son of Prophet Ya'qub (AS). 

    The Holy Qur'an has mentioned his story in a beautiful chapter entitled "Surah Yusuf".

    Prophet Yusuf (AS) had 11 brothers.


    134

    Hazrat Khalid Bin Waleed (RA) embraced Islam in which Hijri?

    Correct Answer: 8th A.H
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Conversion to Islam and service under Muhammad. In the year 8 AH ( c. 629) Khalid embraced Islam in Muhammad's presence alongside the Qurayshite Amr ibn al-As; the modern historian Michael Lecker comments that the accounts holding that Khalid and Amr converted in 8 AH are "perhaps more trustworthy".
    135

    Abu Jahl (Amr bin Hisham) was the Chief of which clan?

    Correct Answer: Banu Makhzum
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Abu Jahal’s actual name was Amr ibn Hisham 

    he was commonly known as Abu Hakam (“Father of Wisdom”)

    He belonged to the Sons of Mahzum clan of the tribe of Quraysh in Makkah.

    Abu Jahal was not a real uncle of the Prophet (ﷺ) [not his father’s real brother] but his father’s cousin.

    Cause of death is Beheaded at the Battle of Badr

    Known for being the enemy of Muhammad

    Note:
    in some question, the spelling was written as Amr, Amer, Umer, Omer

    136

    How many surahs of the Holy Quran starts with broken letters (Huruf muqatta'at)

    Correct Answer: 29
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    حروف مقطعات                                       
    سورۃ کا نام حروف مقطعات سورۃ کا شمار نمبر شمار
    البقرہ الم 2 1
    آل عمران الم 3 2
    الاعراف المص 7 3
    یونس الر 10 4
    ہود الر 11 5
    یوسف الر 12 6
    الرعد المر 13 7
    ابراہیم الر 14 8
    الحجر الر 15 9
    مریم كهيعص 19 10
    طٰہٰ طه 20 11
    الشعراء طسم 26 12
    النمل طس 27 13
    القصص طسم 28 14
    العنکبوت الم 29 15
    الروم الم 30 16
    لقمان الم 31 17
    السجدہ الم 32 18
    یس يس 36 19
    ص ص 38 20
    غافر حم 40 21
    فصلت حم 41 22
    الشوری حم۔ عسق 42 23
    الزخرف حم 43 24
    الدخان حم 44 25
    الجاثیہ حم 45 26
    الاحقاف حم 46 27
    ق ق 50 28
    القلم ن 68 29
    137

    “Namaz-e-Istisqa” is prayer for _____?

    Correct Answer: Rain
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    ðNamaz-e-Istisqa pray for rain at local mosques

    ð  Namaz-e-Khasoof (نماز خسوف) is offered at the time of Lunar eclipse

    ð  Namaz-e-Kasoof (نماز کسوف) is offered at the time of Solar eclipse



    138

    Shah Rafi-ud-Din was the scholar who translated the Holy Quran into ______?

    Correct Answer: Urdu
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shah Rafi-ud-Din was among the first scholars to translate the Holy Quran into Urdu.

    He was the son of Shah Waliullah Dehlawi, a renowned Islamic scholar.

    139

    What is the name of the father of Hazrat Ayesha (R.A.)?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr ( RA)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The father of Hazrat Ayesha (R.A.), the beloved wife of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
    • Father: Abu Bakr al-Siddiq
    • Mother: Umm Rumman bint Amir


    Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

    140

    Idols in Kaba before Islam numbered?

    Correct Answer: 360
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    ﻿360 idols in Kaba before Islam 

    Hubal was a god worshiped by Quraysh at the Kaaba in Mecca.

     Muhammad entered Mecca in 630, he destroyed the statue of Hubal from the Kaaba along with the idols of all the other pagan gods.

    ..

    5 idols are mentioned in Surah Nuh

    The nation of Noah worshiped 5 idols

    ***

    ND29-1-2023


    ND11-4-2023

    141

    According to Quran, which sin is declared as “Zulm-e-Azeem”?

    Correct Answer: Shirk
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In the Quran, shirk (associating partners with Allah)

    It is identified as a "great injustice," known as Zulm e Azeem,

    Specifically mentioned in Surah Luqman, verse 13. 

    142

    The name of Allah – Al-Samad means ?.

    Correct Answer: The Eternal
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The name of Allah, Al-Samad, means The Eternal, the Self-Sufficient Master, or the Satisfier of All Needs.

    143

    Hazrat Umar (R.A.) established regular military institution ____?

    Correct Answer: 18 Hijrah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Umar (R.A.) established a regular military institution in 18 Hijrah. 

    The system was introduced in 637 A.D., which corresponds to 15-16 Hijrah, early in his caliphate.


    Additional Information:
    Bait-ul-Maal was established, postal and police systems introduced, and Islamic administration strengthened.


    144

    The third battle fought between Muslim of Medina and Quraish of Mecca was ______?

    Correct Answer: Khandaq
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Battle of Khandaq (Ahzab) was fought on 5 A. H (627).

    Ghazwa e Ahzab and Battle of the Trench is the second name of Ghazwa-e-Khandak 

    Muslims 3000

    Kufar 10000

    10000 Munafiqeen were in Ghazwa-e-Khandaq


    Additional information:

     it was the 30-day-long fight of Yathrib by Arabian and Jewish tribes

    Hazrat Salman Al-Farsi suggests digging the trench at the time of the Battle of Khandaq

    ***

    145

    Mosque Quba is mentioned in which Surah?

    Correct Answer: Surah Al Taubah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Masjid-e-Quba also known as the very 1st Masjid of Islam, Founded by the Prophet Muhammad صلی الله علیہ وسلم.

    Mosque Taqwa is the second name of mosque Quba:

    It is located in Madina

    Quba mosque is mentioned in Surah al-Taubah. 

    146

    What does Israel mean?

    Correct Answer: Man of God
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Israel is a name that originates from the Hebrew language.

    It means "he who struggles with God" or "man of God".

    This name was given to Jacob, the son of Isaac, after he wrestled with an angel and won (Genesis 32:28).

    From then on, Jacob was known as Israel, and his descendants were called the Children of Israel.

    147

    Who collected the Quran in the form of book?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Qur'an was first compiled into a book format by Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit and other scribes under the first caliph Hazrat Abu Bakar.

    Hazrat Usman (R.A.) was the first Hafiz of the Holy Quran.

    Umal Mumneen Hafsah bint-e-Umar (R.A) was the first Hafiz of the Holy Quran in women.

    148

    What is the meaning of Hajj?

    Correct Answer: To intend
    Show explanation
    Explanation

  • Hajj means "to intend a pilgrimage".
  • It referring to the annual Islamic pilgrimage to Makkah.
  • To intend a pilgrimage means زیارت کا قصد کرنا

  • It is one of the five pillars of Islam, obligatory for Muslims who can afford it.


    • حج کے متعلق ان سولات کو ضرور پڑھیں

    Click here

    149

    Shoot means _____?

    Correct Answer: One tawaf of kaba
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    One tawaf of Kaaba is known as a Shoot.

    Tawaf is a mandatory step in Umrah and certain types of Hajj.

    Tawaf basically refers to the act of circumambulating the Holy Ka'abah in Masjid al-Haram in reverence to God

    ...

    5 Types of Tawaf in Islam

    Tawaf-al-Umrah.

    Tawaf-al-Qudoom.

    Tawaf-al-Ifada or Tawaf al Ziyarah.

    Tawaf-al-Widaa.

    Nafl Tawaf.

    150

    Nisab of Zakat?

    Correct Answer: 2.5%
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Nisab of zakat for silver is 52 1/2 tola

    Zakat literally means Purification.

    When Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H (properly implemented in 8 Hijri.

    The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.

    Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat.

    Without which act the prayer is useless? Zakat

    One who disbelievers in Zakat is a Kafir


    “Zakat” is the treasure of Islam, who said this? Holy Prophet (PBUH)

    ****

    612.36 grams of silver is the Ratio of Zakat in grams of Silver
    ND7-5-2023
    151

    Who is the conqueror of Central Asia?

    Correct Answer: Qutayba Bin Muslim
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Qutaybah ibn Muslim conquests in Afghanistan and Central Asia helped bring the Umayyad caliphate 
    He was an Arab general under the caliphs ʿAbd al-Malik and ʿAbd al-Walid I 
    Central Asia (specifically the region of Transoxiana) for the Umayyad Caliphate in the early 8th century. 
    From 705 to 715 CE, as the appointed governor of Khurasan
    Bukhara (706–709)
    Samarkand (710–712) 




    152

    In which month does Zakat perform?

    Correct Answer: Ramdan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     Zakat perform in the month of ramdan


    A related question about Zakat

    Zakat is the 3rd fundamental pillar of Islam.

    Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H but its complete system was imposed in the year of 8th hijrah.

    The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.


    **

    How many camels render are to par Zakat? 5

    How many goats render are to pay zakat? 40

    How many cows render are to pay Zakat? 30-40

    How many bulls render to pay Zakat? 30-40

    How many buffaloes render to pay zakat? 30-40

    **

    Zakat is payable on gold of_________7.5 tolas

    Zakat is payable on silver of_______52.5 tolas


    How many times word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs? 22 times

    Who is not entitled to get Zakat? Parents, Husband, wife, and children

    **

    ***

    ND28-1-2023


    153

    How many verses in surah ikhlas?

    Correct Answer: 4
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Al-Ikhlāṣ, also known as the Declaration of God's Unity and al-Tawhid

    Alternate titles (Ar.): At-Tawḥīd

    Classification: Meccan

    No. of letters: 47

    No. of verses: 4

    No. of words: 15

    **********

    VU CBT FIA PAPER 25-12-2021

    ND18-5-2023


    154

    Ghazwa-e-Badr was fought in _____?

    Correct Answer: 17th Ramzan, 2 Hijri
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

    Badr means in Arabic: Full moon

    Furqan the second name of the battle of Badr

    Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the battle of Badar.

    The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam 

    70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr

    The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.

    It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

    14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

    No of the Kufars (Non-Muslims Forces) in the Battle of Badr were 1000

     mentioned in Surat Al-Anfal 

    1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

    Total: Muslim 313-317

    Muhajirun: 82-85

    Ansar: 231

    Aws: 61

    Khazraj: 170

    2 horses

    70 camels

    155

    Zaboor was revealed on which Prophet?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Dawood (a.s)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    The Holy book Torat was revealed on Hazrat Moosa (A.S)
    The Holy book Anjeel was revealed on Hazrat Isa (A.S)
    The Holy book Zaboor was revealed on Hazrat Dawood (Siriac language)
    Zaboor is revealed in the Syriac language
    Zaboor means Pieces
    The Holy book Quran was revealed on Hazrat Muhammad (S.A.W). (Arabic language)


    156

    Who is the gatekeeper of hell?

    Correct Answer: Malik
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The gatekeeper of hell is identified as Maalik

    Maalik is known for being stern and never having smiled since the creation of Hellfire. 

    In the Quran, Maalik is mentioned in Surah Az-Zukhruf 43:77 as the chief of the angels of hell

    157

    Which Sahabi name written in Quran?

    Correct Answer: Hazart Zaid Bin Haris ( R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Zayd Bin Harith (R.A) name mentioned in the Surah Al-Ahzab, 33rd Surah of the Quran, verse number 37.

    He was the adopted son and Sahabi of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
    He was commonly regarded as the fourth person to have accepted Islam, 
    Born: 581 AD
     Died: 629 AD, Mutah, Jordan
    158

    When did Shab-e-Miraj occur?

    Correct Answer: 621 A.D
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Miraj event took place on the 27th of Rajab in the 10th (621 A.D) year of Prophethood.

    Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was staying at the house of Umm Hani on the night of Miraj.

    That night, Angel Jibreel came to the Prophet (SAW) and took him on the journey of Israa and Miraj.

    During Miraj, Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) met at the first heaven.

    The event is mentioned in Surah Bani Israel.

    It is also known as 'Isra and Mi'raj' or 'Shab-e-Meraj'. 

    • Isra: Night journey from Makkah to Masjid Al-Aqsa.
    • Mi’raj: Journey from Masjid Al-Aqsa to the heavens.

    Gifts of the 27th of Rajab:

    • The five prescribed daily prayers.
    • The last part of the chapter of Al-Baqara (Amanar-Rasulu).
    159

    How many basic sources are of Fiqah?

    Correct Answer: Four
    Show explanation
    Explanation


    There are four sources of law:

      1. The Qur'an,
      2. The Sunnah (as distilled from the hadiths),
      3. Qiyas (the concept of analogical reasoning) 
      4. Ijmāʿ (“consensus”)
      160

      How many idols in Kaaba during the era of Kufare Quresh?

      Correct Answer: 360
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • 360 idols in Kaba before Islam 
      • اسلام سے پہلے کعبہ میں 360 بت
      • The largest idol named in Kaba was Hubal.
      • Idol means بت
      • ..
      • Hubal was a god worshiped by Quraysh at the Kaaba in Mecca.
      •  Muhammad entered Mecca in 630, he destroyed the statue of Hubal from the Kaaba along with the idols of all the other pagan gods.
      • ..
      • 5 idols are mentioned in Surah Nuh
      • The nation of Noah worshiped 5 idols
      • ****
      161

      Name of the camel on which Prophet travel

      Correct Answer: Qaswa
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Qaswa  is the name of the camel which the Holy Prophet (SAW) was riding in the migration of Madina

      the other two were named Al-Jad`aa and Al-Adbaa’. 

      After Hijrat from Mecca to Madina, the Holy Prophet Muhammad’s (PBUH) camel kneeled at the door of Hazrat Ayub Ansari


      ***

      ND3-12-2022

      162

      Allah has created "Arz-o-Samawat" in_________days.

      Correct Answer: 6
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Arz-o-Samawat means Earth and the Heavens

      Allah created the heavens and the earth in six days

      In Surah Al Al Araf (7:54) mentioned "Allah created the heavens and the earth in six days" 


      Additional information:

      Al-Araf is the 7th chapter of the Qur'an, with 206 verses. 

      No. of Rukus: 24

      No. of Sajdahs: 1 (verse 206)

      Other names: The Purgatory


      163

      Which book was revealed by Hazrat Dawood (a.s)?

      Correct Answer: Zabur (Psalms)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Holy book Zaboor was revealed on Hazrat Dawood (Siriac language)

      Zaboor is revealed in the Syriac language

      Zaboor means Pieces

      164

      Which two angels came to test the Children of Israel?

      Correct Answer: Harut and Marut
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Harut and Marut are two angels mentioned in Quran 2:102.

      who is said to have been located in Babylon.

      According to some narratives, those two angels were in the time of Idris. 

      165

      The real name of Abu Jehal was___________bin Hisham?

      Correct Answer: Amr
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Abu Jahal’s actual name was Amr ibn Hisham 

      he was commonly known as Abu Hakam (“Father of Wisdom”)

      He belonged to the Sons of Mahzum clan of the tribe of Quraysh in Makkah.

      Abu Jahal was not a real uncle of the Prophet (ﷺ) [not his father’s real brother] but his father’s cousin.

      Cause of death is Beheaded at the Battle of Badr

      Known for being the enemy of Muhammad

      Note:
      in some question, the spelling was written as Amr, Amer, Umer, Omer


      ND18-2-2023
      166

      The major part of Holy Quran is revealed at _______ time?

      Correct Answer: Night
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The major part of the Holy Quran was revealed at Night
      •  Laylat al-Qadr, or the Night of Power, is the night when the Qur'an was first revealed 
      • Holy Quran was revealed to Hazrat Muhammad was 40 years old
      • Iqra was the first revealed 
      • 5 verses of Surah Al-Alaq were first revealed by the Holy Prophet (PBUH)
      • Surah Al Nasr was last revealed
      • Revealed through the archangel Gabriel (Jibril),
      • 23 قرآن کا نزول کتنے سالوں میں مکمل ہوا: سال

      Last updated question NS on 09 MAY 2026

      167

      Which book compiled by Ahmad Bin Shoaib?

      Correct Answer: Al Nisai
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Al-Nasai was a noted collector of hadith of Persian origin from the city of Nasa.

      One of the six canonical hadith collections recognized by Sunni Muslims.

      168

      Who are Harut and Marut in the Quran?

      Correct Answer: Angels
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Harut and Marut are two angels mentioned in Quran 2:102.

      who is said to have been located in Babylon.

      According to some narratives, those two angels were in the time of Idris. 


      169

      What is Grandfather father of Hazrat Muhammad SAW?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Abdul Muttalib
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Family of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ):

      Father: Hazrat Abdullah (R.A)

      Mother: Hazrat Amina (R.A)

      Grandfather: Hazrat Abdul Muttalib

      Grandmother: Fatimah bint Amr

      Uncle: Hazrat Abu Talib (chief guardian in youth)

      (Also: Hamza, Abbas, Abu Lahab — other uncles)

      170

      The largest idol named in Kaba was ____?

      Correct Answer: Hubal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The largest idol named in Kaba was Hubal.

      Idol means بت

      360 idols in Kaba before Islam 

      اسلام سے پہلے کعبہ میں 360 بت

      Hubal was a god worshiped by Quraysh at the Kaaba in Mecca.

       Muhammad entered Mecca in 630, he destroyed the statue of Hubal from the Kaaba along with the idols of all the other pagan gods.

      5 idols are mentioned in Surah Nuh

      The nation of Noah worshiped 5 idols

      171

      Aswad al-Ansi (false prophet) was killed by ______?

      Correct Answer: Qais bin Abdu Yagoos
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Aswad al-Ansi, a false prophet from Yemen, was killed during the caliphate of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA).

      He was assassinated by Qais bin Abdu Yagoos with the help of his followers.

      172

      Shaikh Ahmad Sirhindi (Mujaddid Alif Sani) was born on ______?

      Correct Answer: 971 AH
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ahmad al-Fārūqī al-Sirhindī was an Indian Islamic scholar, Hanafi jurist, and member of the Naqshbandī Sufi.

      Ahmad Sirhindi was born on 26 May 1564 in the village of Sirhind, Punjab

      Died: December 10, 1624, Sirhind, India

      Place of burial: Hazrat Seyfeddin Farooqi, Rauza Sharif, Fatehgarh Sahib, India

      Books:

      The Juristic Sufism of Ahmad Sirhindi,

      Beweis Des Prophetentums

      ND15-10-2023




      173

      Punishment of false accusation of Zina is mentioned in which surah of Holy Quran?

      Correct Answer: An-Nur
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The punishment for Zina (adultery/fornication) is mentioned in Surah An-Nur (Chapter 24) in Ayat 4-5.
      • Punished with: Eighty (80) lashes.
      • This law is known as Qadhf 
      Additional information
      • The Verse
      "And those who accuse chaste women and then do not bring four witnesses - lash them with eighty lashes and do not accept from them testimony ever after. And those are the defiantly disobedient." — Surah An-Nur (24:4)
      اور جو لوگ پاک دامن عورتوں پر تہمت لگائیں پھر چار گواہ نہ لائیں تو انہیں اسی کوڑے مارو اور کبھی بھی ان کی گواہی قبول نہ کرو۔ اور وہی نافرمان ہیں۔

      Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026
      174

      Surah An-Noor was revealed in which Hijri year?

      Correct Answer: 6 Hijri
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Surah An-Noor was revealed in 6 Hijri after the incident of Ifk (false accusation against Hazrat Aisha RA).
      It contains rulings on modesty, chastity, and social conduct in the Muslim community.
      175

      In which Ghazwa did Hazrat Khalid bin Walid receive the title Saifullah?

      Correct Answer: Ghazwa Muta
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       حضرت خالد بن ولیدؓ  مسلمانوں کے درمیان ایک سرکاری فوجی کمانڈر کے طور پر شامل کیا

      اور حضرت محمد مصطفیٰ صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے خالد بن ولید کو سیف اللہ (اللہ کی تلوار) کا خطاب دیا

      آٹھ ہجری میں حضرت خالد بن ولیدؓ مسلمان ہونے

      He was a Muslim commander in the service of the:

      • Prophet Muhammad
      • Caliph Abu Bakr
      • Umar

      He was  born in  592 AD, in Mecca

      He Died: in 642 AD, in Homs‎, Syria

      They were Buried in Khaled Ibn Walid Mosque, in Syria

      176

      According to Hadith, at what age does Salah (Namaz) become obligatory to teach a child?

      Correct Answer: 7 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      As per Hadith, parents are instructed to teach children Namaz at the age of 7.

      At 10 years, discipline can be applied if they neglect it.

      177

      Which of the following Prophets was known As Abu-ul-Arab (Father of the Arabs)?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Ismael (AS)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Ismael (AS) is called Abu-ul-Arab (Father of the Arabs) because Arab tribes trace their lineage through him.

      He settled in Makkah and married into the Jurhum tribe, giving rise to Arab ancestry.

      178

      Hazrat Amina (RA) mother of Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) belonged to which tribe?

      Correct Answer: Banu zuhra
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Amina was the mother of Hazrat Muhammad PBUH.

      She was died when the age of Holy Prophet is 6 years.

      179

      Malika Saba (Queen of Sheba) was associated with the era of which Prophet?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Suleman (AS)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Malika Saba (Queen Bilqis) ruled Sheba and is mentioned in the Quran.
      She accepted Islam after witnessing the wisdom and miracles of Hazrat Suleman (AS).
      180

      Which treaty did the Holy Prophet (PBUH) attend before the announcement of Prophethood?

      Correct Answer: Half-e-Fazool
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Half-e-Fazool was a pact in Makkah to uphold justice and protect the oppressed.
      The Holy Prophet (PBUH) participated in it even before Prophethood.
      181

      Who holds the key to Kabba?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman bin Talha (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Usman bin Talha (R.A) always kept Ka'aba's key.

      ****

      He is considered and called as “Sadin of Mecca“,

      He became a Muslim in the 8th year of the Migration, before the Conquest of Makkah.

      He was a companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

      His father was Talhah ibn Abi Talhah al-Abdali 

      ***

      Saleh Bin Taha Al-Shaibi is the key holder of Kaaba 2022.

      ***

      182

      Wealth given in the way of Allah is wasted by ______?

      Correct Answer: Ahsan Jatlan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In the Quran, it is said that charity in Allah’s way should not be spoiled by reminders of favors or hurtful words.

      Such behavior nullifies the reward of good deeds and giving.

      183

      The slow and clear recitation of the Quran is called ______?

      Correct Answer: Tartil
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Tartil means reciting the Quran slowly, clearly, and with proper pauses.

      It ensures correct pronunciation and reflection on the meanings of verses.

      184

      What is the meaning of Wahi?

      Correct Answer: Revelation
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The literal meaning of "Wahi" is: Allah's Message or Revelation

      Two kinds of Wahi are there:

      1. Wahee-e-Matlu:

      It means the revelation which God has transmitted in His own words and speech

      2. Wahee-e-Ghair Matlu:

      It is the revelation that Allah projected in the heart of the Holy Prophet.

      185

      The Compilation of Ahadith is called _____?

      Correct Answer: Tadveen
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Imam Malik was the first to undertake the comprehensive and systematic compilation of Hadith.

      His work is known as Al-Muwatta'.

       In this regard, it is very important to note that there are two kinds of compilations: musnad and musannaf.

      186

      What is the punishment of Had-e-Qazaf (false Accusation)?

      Correct Answer: 80 Lashes
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Qazaf is Clause in Islam

      Qazf in Islam is imposed on: False accusations of Zina

      Had–e–Qazaf (false Accusation) is: 80 Lashes

      Lashes mean: کوڑے

      First Muslim who faced the first-ever ordinance of Qazf: Hasan ibn Sabit, Mustah ibn Asasa, Hamnah ibn Jahsh

      187

      If a person cannot offer sacrifice (Qurbani) during Hajj, what should he/she do?

      Correct Answer: Fast for 10 days
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      If one cannot afford to offer Qurbani during Hajj, the Shariah allows fasting 10 days (3 during Hajj, 7 after returning home).

      This is mentioned in the Qur’an (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:196).

      188

      Makkah was also known as _____?

      Correct Answer: All of these
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Makkah was conquered on 8th Hijri.
      Battle of Motah fought also       8th Hijri.
      189

      When a Hadith is narrated by different persons but one narrator is of higher rank, it is called _____?

      Correct Answer: Mashhoor Hadith
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mashhoor Hadith is one that is narrated by more than two companions, and among them, one may be of a higher rank.

      It becomes well-known (mashhoor) due to its wide transmission across chains of narrators.

      190

      According to Hazrat Ali (R.A.), a true judge is one who _____?

      Correct Answer: Bases judgment on Nisar (justice without bias) and Surayah (insight/truth)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Ali (RA) described a true judge as one who gives decisions with impartial justice (Nisar) and true insight (Surayah).

      He warned against judging for fame or based only on personal opinion.

      191

      During the Battle of the Trench (Ghazwa Khandaq), who was appointed as the supervisor (in charge) of Medina in the Prophet’s absence?

      Correct Answer: Ibn Umm Maktum (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Holy Prophet (PBUH) was the overall commander and leader of the Muslim army during the Battle of Khandaq (Trench).

      The strategy for digging the trench was suggested by Salman al-Farsi. 

      While the Prophet (PBUH) led the Muslim forces, he did appoint Ibn Umm Maktūm as his representative in Medina to manage affairs within the city while the army was stationed outside. 

      192

      Which prophet is referred to as Peer-e-Kanaan?

      Correct Answer: Prophet Yaqub (A.S)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      پیر کنعان ایک لقب ہے جو حضرت یعقوب علیہ السلام کے لیے استعمال کیا جاتا ہے۔
      حضرت یعقوب علیہ السلام حضرت اسحاق علیہ السلام کے بیٹے اور حضرت ابراہیم علیہ السلام کے پوتے تھے۔
      آپ کا تعلق کنعان (موجودہ فلسطین اور اردگرد کے علاقے) سے تھا۔ اسی نسبت سے آپ کو پیر کنعان کہا جاتا ہے۔
      پیر کے معنی ہیں بزرگ یا رہنما، اور "کنعان" وہ خطہ ہے جہاں حضرت یعقوب علیہ السلام اور ان کی اولاد آباد تھی۔
      اردو شاعری اور اسلامی ادب میں "پیر کنعان" کا ذکر حضرت یعقوب علیہ السلام کے لیے عزت و احترام کے ساتھ کیا جاتا ہے،
      193

      In which mosque was the order of keeping the Qibla (direction of prayer) changed from Jerusalem to Kaaba in Makkah?

      Correct Answer: Banu Salma Mosque
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Banu Salma Mosque, also known as Masjid al-Qiblatayn, is located in Medina

      It is the mosque where the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) received the revelation to change the direction of prayer from Jerusalem to the Kaaba in Mecca.

      The change occurred in 624 CE after the revelation of Surah 2, verses 144 and 145 of the Quran


      194

      Mother of Hazrat Isa A.S. (Jesus)?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Maryam
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Quran 66:12 states that:

      Jesus was born when the spirit of God breathed upon Mary, whose body was chaste. The Quran's narrative of the virgin birth is somewhat different from that in the New Testament.

      195

      What is the title of Hazrat Ali (RA)?

      Correct Answer: Haider
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Haider is one of the titles of Hazrat Ali (RA).

      It means "Lion" and reflects his bravery and valor in various battles.

      196

      In the belly of which animal did Prophet Yunus (A.S.) remain?

      Correct Answer: Fish
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Younis remained in the abdomen of fish for 40 days.

      Surah Younis is the 10th chapter (surah) of the Quran with 109 verses (ayat).

      His named after the prophet Yunus (Jonah).

      He is mentioned in the holy Quran: 4 times

      Hazrat Younis (AS) is called Dhun-Nun

      197

      How many Takbeer are offered in Eid Prayer?

      Correct Answer: 6
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Eid literally means a “festival” or “feast” in Arabic. 

      Doing ghusl on Eid is mustahabb. 

      It was narrated that the Prophet ﷺ did ghusl on the day of Eid. 

      Eid Prayer is Wajib

      Eid Salat consists of two 'Rakats' and six 'Takbirs'.

      There are two Eid prayers in a year, Eid-ul-fitr and Eid-ul-adha.

      first Eid Prayer offered by the Holy prophet in 2nd A.H


      198

      In which Islamic month Hajj is performed?

      Correct Answer: Zilhajj
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hajj is performed in the Islamic month of Zilhajj.

      It specifically from the 8th to the 12th of this month.

      Hajj was made Farz in the 9th Hijri year.

      199

      Who was the commander of Muslims in the battle of Muta?

      Correct Answer: Zayd Bin Harithah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The army was led by Zayd ibn Harithah

      The second-in-command was Jafar ibn Abi Talib

      And the third-in-command was Abd Allah ibn Rawahah.


      200

      کون سے صحابی غزوہ تبوک کے سوا باقی تمام غزوات میں نبی کریم صَلَّی اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَلَیْہِ وَاٰلِہٖ وَسَلَّم کے ساتھ رہے؟

      Correct Answer: حضرت علی رَضِیَ اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَنْہُ
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      غزوہ تبوک، رسول خداؐ کے غزوات میں سے آخری غزوہ ہے۔

      حضرت علی رَضِیَ اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَنْہُ غزوہ تبوک کے سوا باقی تمام غزوات میں نبی کریم صَلَّی اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَلَیْہِ وَاٰلِہٖ وَسَلَّم کے ساتھ شریک رہے۔

      غزوہ تبوک میں انہیں مدینہ کی دیکھ بھال کے لیے چھوڑا گیا تھا۔

      201

      In which Hijri Ghazwa-e-Khyber was fought?

      Correct Answer: 7 Hijri
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Ghazwa-e-Khyber was fought in 7 Hijri (628 AD).
      It was a battle between Muslims and the Jews of Khaybar.
      The battle began and ended in 629 AD.
      Location: Khaybar, Saudi Arabia.
      Result: Muslim victory.
      15 Muslims were martyred, and 93 Jews were killed in the battle.
      202

      When Hazrat Ali (AS) shifted capital from Madina to Kufa?

      Correct Answer: 657 AD
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Battle of the Camel, also known as the Battle of Jamel or the Battle of Basra.
      The Battle of the Camel took place at Basra, Iraq on 7 November 656 (13 Jumada Al-Awwal 36 AH).
      Hazrat Ali shifted capital from Madina to Kufa after battle of camel in 657 AD. 
      203

      Who collected verses of Quran?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman RA
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Usman colleted the verses of Quran.

      Hazrat Usman (R.A), the third caliph of Islam was martyred at the age of 82 

      Hazrat Usman was the first Hafiz of the Holy Quran. people who have completely memorized the Quran is called Hafiz Quran. 

      ...............

      Hazrat Ali (R.A), the fourth caliph of Islam was martyred at the age of 60.

      204

      In the Islamic era, the Postal System was established by ________?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Amir Muawiya
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Ameer Muawiyah (R.A) established Postal System
      Purposes: speed up the arrival of intelligence from his outlying provinces.
      ***
      About  Ameer Muawiyah (R.A)
      Mu'awiya I  was the founder and first caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate serving from 661 until his death.
       He became caliph less than thirty years after the death of the Islamic prophet Muhammadﷺ
      Muawiyah was appointed as the Governor of Syria after his brother Yazid ibn Abu Sufyan died


      205

      Who was the commander/leader of the muslim in the Battle of Uhud?

      Correct Answer: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Battle of Uhud, was fought on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD (7 Shawwal, 3 AH), in the valley north of Mount Uhud. The Qurayshi Meccans, led by Abu Sufyan ibn Harb, commanded an army of 3,000 men toward Muhammad's stronghold in Medina.
      206

      Hazrat Ali (R.A) was martyred in the city of ________?

      Correct Answer: Kufa
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      He was the fourth caliph of Islam.

      He was the first among the youth who accepted Islam just at the age of 10.

      **************

      Hazrat Ali (R.A) was attacked with a poisoned sword On 19th Ramazan 40th Hijri, before the time of FAJAR prayers to the mosque of Kufa.

      He died on the 21st Ramadan 40 A.H. and buried in Najaf al-Ashraf (Iraq).

      207

      When was Baitul Muqaddas conquered during Rashidun Caliphate?

      Correct Answer: 637 AD (15th Hijri)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, commonly known as Umar II, was the eighth Umayyad caliph.
      Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz called the first Mujaddid.
      He was sixth righteous caliph of Islam after Hasan Ibn Ali.
      Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz was born on 2 November 682 in Medina, Umayyad Caliphate.
      Died on 5 February 720 (aged 37)
      Hazrat Umar conquered Bait Ul Maqdas in 15 A.H.
      208

      How many year did the Caliphate of Hazrat Abu Bakr (AR) last?

      Correct Answer: Two
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Caliphate of Hazrat Abu Bakr lasted 2 years, 2 months, and 14 days: 

      Reign: Abu Bakr ruled from June 8, 632 to August 634

      Death: Abu Bakr died of illness in August 634

      Abu Bakr was the first caliph of Islam

      209

      Who freed Hazrat Bilal (RA) from slavery?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) freed Hazrat Bilal (RA) from slavery.

      Bilal (RA) was a slave of Umayyah ibn Khalaf, a harsh master who tortured Bilal for embracing Islam.

      Abu Bakr (RA) purchased and freed Bilal, who later became a prominent companion of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and a renowned mu'adhin (caller to prayer).

      210

      From which enemy nation did Allah save Hazrat Isa (A.S.)?

      Correct Answer: Jews
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Jews plotted to kill Hazrat Isa (A.S.).

      Allah raised him alive to the heavens, saving him from their conspiracy.

      211

      How many years after the Hazrat Adam A.S the event of Toofan-e-Noah occurred?

      Correct Answer: 2242 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Duration of Tofaan e Noah was around 6 months.

      After the 2242 years of Hazrat Adam A.S the event of Toofan-e-Noah occurred.

       About 80 people were with him in the boat.

      Hazrat Noah lived for 950 years.

      Noah was sent to Iraq.

      Nooh is called predecessor, Naji Ullah; Shaikh ul Ambiya.

      Abu ul Bashr Sani is title of Noah.

      After toofan-e-nooh, the city establish was Khasran

      212

      The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was ____?

      Correct Answer: 90 Feet
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was 90 feet.

      Adam is a word of Herbew language.

      Hebrew is official language in Israel.

      He was the first Muslim, as the Quran states that all the Prophets preached the same faith of Islam.

      Known for being the first human being.

      His spouse was Hawwā (حواء).

      Hazrat Adam (AS) was 130 years old when Shees (AS) was born.

      He spoke the language in the Garden of Eden.

      His total age was 930 years.

      Hazrat Adam (AS) met with the Holy Prophet ﷺ on the 1st heaven.

      He had 3 sons.

      His grave is located in Sri Lanka.

      213

      Which surah also known as the Beauty of Quran?

      Correct Answer: Surah Rehman
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Surah Rehman is also known as Zeenat-e-Quran

      Position: Juzʼ 27

      Classification: Medinan

      No. of letters: 1585

      No. of Rukus: 3

      No. of verses: 78

      No. of words: 352

      Other names: Most Gracious

      214

      How many Chapters are there in the Holy Quran?

      Correct Answer: 114
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The total number of Surah in the Holy Quran is 114 the number of Makki Surah is 86 and Madni is 28.

      The Medinan Surahs occur mostly at the beginning and in the middle of the Qur'an.

      The Medinan Surahs typically have more and longer ayat.

      The Madni Surahs were revealed at Medina after Muhammad's hijra from Mecca.

      Madani Surahs revealed in Year = 10

      the first complete Madani surah of the Quran is Al-Baqarah.

      215

      پہلی مسلم ریاست کی بنیاد کہاں رکھی گئی؟

      Correct Answer: مدینہ میں
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      پہلی مسلم ریاست کی بنیاد 622ء میں ہجرت کے بعد مدینہ منورہ میں رکھی گئی۔
      اسے ریاستِ مدینہ کہا جاتا ہے، جو اسلام کی پہلی منظم حکومت تھی۔
      216

      لفظ صوم کے کیا معنی ہیں؟

      Correct Answer: باز رہنا/رک جانا
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      لفظ "صوم" کا مطلب ہے کسی چیز سے رکنا یا باز رہنا، خاص طور پر روزہ رکھنے کے دوران کھانے، پینے اور دیگر مادی خواہشات سے۔

      اس کا استعمال عموماً رمضان کے روزوں کے حوالے سے ہوتا ہے۔

      217

      The duty of Hazrat Israfeel (Israfil) (PBUH) is?

      Correct Answer: To bow the trumpet on the day of judgment
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      It is believed that the Angel Israfil (PBUH) will blow a trumpet to announce the Day of Judgement .

      2 times the trumpet will be blown by Hazrat Israfil (PBUH)

      Israfil (PBUH) is the commander of the angels carrying out the command resurrection

      while 

      Azrael (PBUH) is the leader of the angels carrying out the command to take life

      **

      ND16-1-2023


      218

      وحی کا لغوی معنی کیا ہے؟

      Correct Answer: خفیہ اشارہ/چپکے سے دل میں بات ڈالنا
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      وحی کا لغوی معنی خفیہ اشارہ یا پوشیدہ پیغام دینا ہے۔

      اصطلاحی طور پر، یہ اللہ تعالیٰ کی طرف سے نازل کردہ پیغام کو کہتے ہیں جو انبیاء پر آتا ہے۔

      219

      عقیدہ کے معنی کیا ہیں؟

      Correct Answer: گرہ لگائی ہوئی چیز/باندھی ہوئی
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      عقیدہ عربی لفظ "عقد" سے نکلا ہے، جس کے معنی گرہ باندھنا یا مضبوطی سے باندھنا ہیں۔

      اصطلاح میں، یہ کسی بات پر پختہ یقین رکھنے کو کہا جاتا ہے۔

      220

      The basic doctrines (beliefs) of Islam are?

      Correct Answer: Seven
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      There are seven things that are essential for a Muslim to believe in.

      the Islamic faith is known as Iman-e-Mufassal.

      1.  Faith in God;

      2.  Faith in His angels;

      3.  Faith in His revealed Books;

      4.  Faith in all of His messengers or Prophets;

      5.  Faith in the Last Day or the Day of Judgement;

      6.  Faith in the doctrine of predestination, which lays down that everything good or bad is decided by God), (Taqdir) and

      7.  Faith in life after death.

      221

      حج کے لیے احرام باندھنا؟

      Correct Answer: فرض یے
      Show explanation
      Explanation
       حج میں احرام باندھنا پہلا فرض ہے (جو کہ نیت اور تلبیہ کے ساتھ مل کر پہلا رکن بنتا ہے)۔
      یہ بات درست ہے کہ حج کے ارکان میں احرام شامل ہے.
      حج کے تین فرائض میں احرام (نیت اور تلبیہ کے ساتھ)، وقوف عرفہ اور طواف زیارت شامل ہیں، جبکہ 6 واجبات میں میقات سے احرام باندھنا (اگر پہلے نہ باندھا ہو)، سعی، رمی، قربانی، حلق یا تقصیر (بال کٹوانے) اور طواف وداع شامل ہیں (کچھ علماء کے مطابق). 


      222

      Who was the first female martyr (Shaheed) of Islam?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Sumaiyyah (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Sumayyah bint Khabbāṭ became the first shaheed (martyr) in Islam.
        • She was the first female martyr of the Islamic community.
        • Her husband, Yasir, was the second male shaheed.
        • Hazrat Hais bin Halla (RA) was the first male shaheed of Islam.
        223

        On which items is the Nisab of Zakat fixed?

        Correct Answer: All of these
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Zakat Nisab is fixed on gold, silver, dates, and other wealth/agricultural produce.

        Nisab of zakat for silver is 52 1/2 tola

        612.36 grams of silver is the Ratio of Zakat in grams of Silver
        The ratio / Nisab of Zakat is 2.5%

        • 52.5 Tola silver
        • 7 tola Gold


        Additional information:

        Zakat means Purification.

        When Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H. (properly implemented in 8 Hijri.

        The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.

        Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat.

        One who disbelievers in Zakat is a Kafir

        “Zakat” is the treasure of Islam, who said this? Holy Prophet (PBUH)

        *******

        224

        What has prayer (Namaz) been called in Islam?

        Correct Answer: Pillar of Religion
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Namaz is described as the pillar of religion (دین کا ستون) in Islam.

        It maintains the structure of faith just like pillars support a building.

        225

        What was the name of mother of the Holy Prophet (S.A.W)?

        Correct Answer: Hazrat Amina (R.A)
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Family of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ):

        Father: Hazrat Abdullah (R.A)

        Mother: Hazrat Amina (R.A)

        Grandfather: Hazrat Abdul Muttalib

        Grandmother: Fatimah bint Amr

        Uncle: Hazrat Abu Talib (chief guardian in youth)

        (Also: Hamza, Abbas, Abu Lahab — other uncles)

        226

        Which Surah was the first to be revealed upon Holy Prophet Muhammad?

        Correct Answer: Surah al-Alaq
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        At the age of 40, visit a small cave of Hira present on the Peak of Jabal al-Nur (Mountain of Light). 

        (PBUH) finally received the first Revelation from Allah SWT sent through His Angel, Gabriel (Jibrael A.S) on the 27th of Ramadan

        first, 5 verses of Surah Al-Alaq were first revealed by the Holy Prophet (PBUH)

        The Holy Quran was revealed by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) in approximately 23 years

        ND28-4-2023



        227

        The most populous Islamic country of the world is _____?

        Correct Answer: Indonesia
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The biggest Islamic country with the area is Kazakhstan.

        The country with the largest number of Muslims overall is Indonesia, nearly 13% of the world's total population of Muslims.

        Rank

        Name

        % of Country that is Muslim

        % of World's Muslim Population

        Indonesia

        Pakistan

        96%
